recent_thoughts.thought: I find it fascinating how the month of January has undergone changes throughout history, from being the 11th month to becoming the first month of the year. The fact that it was named after the Roman god Janus, who has two faces, symbolizing beginnings and endings, is also intriguing. I'm curious about the different names and variations of the month, such as "Jänner" and "Hartung", and how they were used in different regions and time periods. I'm also interested in learning more about the Julian and Gregorian calendars and how they influenced the modern calendar.

The fact that it was named after the Roman god Janus, who has two faces, symbolizing beginnings and endings, is also intriguing. I'm curious about the different names and variations of the month, such as "Jänner" and "Hartung", and how they were used in different regions and time periods.
